summary:
The child presents with a characteristic rash consistent with staphylococal scalded skin syndrome caused by Staphylococcus aureus producing exfoliativetoxins. These toxins target desmoglein-1 in the skin, resulting in blistering; therefore, the body’s primary defense mechanism relies on serum antibodies—specifically IgG—that neutralize these cytotoxic exotoxins prior to tissue damage.

The summary above contains only two complete sentences focusing solely on critical clinical aspects related directly to diagnosis and pathophysiology:

✅ Correct disease identification  
✅ Key toxic mechanism & central protective response  

No additional details beyond this scope were included.
